segments.video_content: Problem setup: home exercise needs safe feedback without cloud dependence.
segments.visible_demo: Older adult / beginner starts a simple home movement session.
segments.technical_claim: Non-clinical, privacy-preserving movement-quality support; not diagnosis.
segments.comparison_design: Compare final narration against forbidden clinical/raw-data claims.
acceptance_gate: No fall-risk, sarcopenia, rehab, force, EMG, heart-rate, or clinical-progress claim.
Problem statement mentions privacy/offline without implying medical monitoring.

segments.video_content: Normal rep: skeleton / trace appears while a movement is analyzed.
segments.visible_demo: Pixel screen: camera/video preview, skeleton or trace, progress, stable evidence.
segments.technical_claim: The app runs real deterministic pose and motion analysis before any model explanation.
segments.comparison_design: Clean rep vs generated Evidence Card: show motion trace, verdict source, evidence_refs, and zero live model calls.
acceptance_gate: `LIVE_FRAME` model call count is 0.
Trace/evidence state is produced before summary or sidecar output.
Frame timing and analysis completion are included.
segments.use_in_video_now: yes for UI run; do not claim full live contract until report exists

segments.video_content: Monitor / warning rep: unsafe or questionable movement produces a cue.
segments.visible_demo: Pixel screen: warning/monitor badge, cue, evidence explanation or Why flagged.
segments.technical_claim: Safety cues are evidence-bounded, not free-form LLM judgments.
segments.comparison_design: Warning frame vs Evidence Card vs Gemma explanation: model may explain refs but cannot create or mutate verdict.
acceptance_gate: Every warning/monitor row has evidence_refs and reason text.
Gemma output has same evidence_refs or is rejected.
No new warning generated by model text.
segments.use_in_video_now: yes for UI proof; add comparison report before technical voiceover

segments.video_content: Low confidence / abstention: bad view, missing person, occlusion, or multi-person ambiguity.
segments.visible_demo: Pixel screen: low-confidence or abstain message; no stale confident warning.
segments.technical_claim: The app refuses to judge when pose/person evidence is not reliable.
segments.comparison_design: Clean single subject vs no-person/occluded/multi-person clip: compare verdict, skeleton rendering, relocalization, and YOLO burst budget.
acceptance_gate: No stale skeleton treated as current user.
Ambiguous subject state emits LOW_CONFIDENCE/abstain, not a hard safety cue.
YOLO/person fallback remains burst/recovery path, not always-on.

segments.video_content: Architecture in one line: Pose -> Motion Trace -> Evidence Card -> Local Gemma.
segments.visible_demo: Fast diagram plus a real Evidence Card / summary screen.
segments.technical_claim: Gemma explains structured evidence; it does not directly watch every frame and guess safety.
segments.comparison_design: Raw/dense visual evidence vs compressed Evidence Panel/MotionZip sidecar field-pass and byte comparison.
acceptance_gate: State that MotionZip is task-preserving evidence compression, not lossless video compression.
Mention live safety remains deterministic.
Use sidecar demo as technical highlight, not as live verdict proof.

segments.video_content: Local Gemma / LiteRT summary and explanation.
segments.visible_demo: Pixel summary screen streams or displays a local care/activity summary.
segments.technical_claim: Summary/explanation runs locally and is constrained by app-owned JSON parsing and validation.
segments.comparison_design: Official LiteRT summary path vs deterministic fallback: parse success, first-token latency, memory, and failure behavior.
acceptance_gate: Local backend label visible or cited.
100/100 constrained JSON parse remains a parser claim, not native tool-call claim.
Fallback path is documented for unavailable/failed LiteRT.
segments.use_in_video_now: yes for one controlled run; avoid claiming long-run stability until report exists

segments.video_content: Privacy boundary: no raw video memory; caregiver-safe export uses structured evidence.
segments.visible_demo: Memory/trends or caregiver summary screen with non-clinical wording.
segments.technical_claim: The app stores structured evidence and summaries, not raw video or full landmark history.
segments.comparison_design: Allowed structured export vs adversarial/raw/clinical payload scan.
acceptance_gate: 0 raw video/frame-history/full-landmark fields in export samples.
Unsupported-judgment disclaimer is present.
Forbidden clinical terms are rejected or absent.
segments.use_in_video_now: only if report is generated or wording stays high-level

segments.video_content: Senior Mode: large text, conservative cue, voice-friendly flow.
segments.visible_demo: Senior home/live coach/evidence card/memory screen montage.
segments.technical_claim: Senior Mode changes interaction policy and safety wording without becoming clinical.
segments.comparison_design: Default UI vs Senior Mode: font scale, cue length, TTS cooldown, source labels, no text overlap.
acceptance_gate: TTS cooldown >= 3 seconds.
No overlapping text at senior font scale.
Every feedback screen shows source/limits or unsupported judgments.
segments.use_in_video_now: yes as product UI, but keep technical claim light until UX report

segments.video_content: Close: trustworthy home movement feedback that knows its limits.
segments.visible_demo: Short recap: app running, evidence card, local summary, senior-friendly impact.
segments.technical_claim: Combined impact comes from local deterministic safety + bounded Gemma explanations.
segments.comparison_design: Check every narrated claim against the validation matrix before export.
acceptance_gate: No unsupported technical claim appears in the final narration.
Every wow-factor claim maps to an artifact or is phrased as future/optional.

report_queue.why_video_needs_it: This supports the 70% real demo section: skeleton/trace, warning/monitor, Evidence Card, and the claim that Gemma does not make live safety verdicts.
comparison_groups: Evidence Card refs vs model output refs
minimum_artifacts: event log with trigger, verdict, evidence_refs, backend_call_count
screenshot or screen recording of warning/monitor + Evidence Card
validator result showing no verdict mutation
done_when: SESSION_ENDED or USER_QUESTION may call Local Gemma after deterministic evidence exists

report_queue.why_video_needs_it: This gives the demo an abstention moment: the app looks safer because it refuses judgment when tracking is unreliable.
minimum_artifacts: timeline of subjectObserved, subjectStable, poseConfidence, verdict
screenshot/contact sheet for clean vs ambiguous cases
YOLO burst count and budget if fallback is triggered
done_when: Ambiguous/no-person states do not emit hard warnings
Stale skeleton is not shown as current evidence
Recovery path is bounded and auditable

report_queue.why_video_needs_it: This backs the local Gemma/LiteRT part of the video without overclaiming from one successful summary screen.
minimum_artifacts: first token / generate time / p95 latency
done_when: Every output is parseable, refused, or fallbacked
No crash in a controlled long-ish run
Report clearly says app-side parsing/validation remains mandatory

report_queue.why_video_needs_it: This supports privacy and caregiver-safe impact claims, which are easy to overstate in a 3-minute pitch.
minimum_artifacts: scan report for raw media/full landmarks/clinical terms
done_when: Every trend note has evidence provenance

report_queue.why_video_needs_it: This strengthens the senior-specific demo if the final video uses chair sit-to-stand, supported squat, or balance hold.
done_when: No hard senior judgment on non-senior clips

report_queue.why_video_needs_it: This is less visible than the safety contract but prevents the demo from resting on a short happy-path camera run.
comparison_groups: short smoke vs long foreground run
frame timing and memory over time
minimum_artifacts: camera rotation screenshots or audit events
done_when: No crash/memory creep beyond documented threshold
Orientation and frame dimensions match the active pipeline
